title: Alex Acuña

infobox.value: Alejandro Neciosup Acuña   (1944-12-12) December 12, 1944 (age 81)  [Pativilca](/wiki/Pativilca "Pativilca"), [Barranca](/wiki/Barranca_Province "Barranca Province"), Peru

* *[Weather Report](/wiki/Weather_Report_(1971_album) "Weather Report (1971 album)")* (1971) * *[I Sing the Body Electric](/wiki/I_Sing_the_Body_Electric_(album) "I Sing the Body Electric (album)")* (1972) * *[Sweetnighter](/wiki/Sweetnighter "Sweetnighter")* (1973) * *[Mysterious Traveller](/wiki/Mysterious_Traveller "Mysterious Traveller")* (1974) * *[Tale Spinnin'](/wiki/Tale_Spinnin%27 "Tale Spinnin'")* (1975) * *[Black Market](/wiki/Black_Market_(Weather_Report_album) "Black Market (Weather Report album)")* (1976) * *[Heavy Weather](/wiki/Heavy_Weather_(album) "Heavy Weather (album)")* (1977) * *[Mr.

(born December 12, 1944), known professionally as **Alex Acuña**, is a [Peruvian–American](/wiki/Peruvian_Americans "Peruvian Americans") [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") drummer and percussionist.[[1]](#cite_note-New_Grove-1) He has also worked as an educator at [University of California, Los Angeles](/wiki/University_of_California,_Los_Angeles "University of California, Los Angeles"), and [Berklee College of Music](/wiki/Berklee_College_of_Music "Berklee College of Music"). LAMA, [Musicians Institute](/wiki/Musicians_Institute "Musicians Institute"), USC, CSUN.

Collins, Catherine; Kernfeld, Barry (2002). "Acuña, Alex". In Barry Kernfeld (ed.). *The new Grove dictionary of jazz* (2nd ed.). New York: Grove's Dictionaries Inc. pp. 11–12. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [1561592846](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1561592846 "Special:BookSources/1561592846").

*The tide was always high : the music of Latin America in Los Angeles*. Josh Kun, Pacific Standard Time: LA/LA. Oakland, California. 2017. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-0-520-29439-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-520-29439-4 "Special:BookSources/978-0-520-29439-4"). [OCLC](/wiki/OCLC_(identifier) "OCLC (identifier)") [974992156](https://search.worldcat.org/oclc/974992156).`{{cite book}}`: CS1 maint: location missing publisher ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_location_missing_publisher "Category:CS1 maint: location missing publisher")) CS1 maint: others ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_others "Category:CS1 maint: others"))

[*The Tide Was Always High: The Music of Latin America in Los Angeles*](https://www.jstor.org/stable/10.1525/j.ctt1trkkk3) (1 ed.). University of California Press. 2017. [JSTOR](/wiki/JSTOR_(identifier) "JSTOR (identifier)") [10.1525/j.ctt1trkkk3](https://www.jstor.org/stable/10.1525/j.ctt1trkkk3).

Peruvian drummer and percussionist

| Alex Acuña | |
| --- | --- |
| [Acuña in 2010](/wiki/File:Alex_Acu%C3%B1a_(5022184811).jpg "Acuña in 2010") Acuña in 2010 | |
| Background information | |

| Genres | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz"), [jazz fusion](/wiki/Jazz_fusion "Jazz fusion"), [Afro-Cuban jazz](/wiki/Afro-Cuban_jazz "Afro-Cuban jazz"), pop |

Musical artist

**Alejandro Neciosup Acuña** (born December 12, 1944), known professionally as **Alex Acuña**, is a [Peruvian–American](/wiki/Peruvian_Americans "Peruvian Americans") [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") drummer and percussionist.[[1]](#cite_note-New_Grove-1) He has also worked as an educator at [University of California, Los Angeles](/wiki/University_of_California,_Los_Angeles "University of California, Los Angeles"), and [Berklee College of Music](/wiki/Berklee_College_of_Music "Berklee College of Music"). LAMA, [Musicians Institute](/wiki/Musicians_Institute "Musicians Institute"), USC, CSUN.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alex_Acu%C3%B1a&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Born in [Pativilca](/wiki/Pativilca "Pativilca"), Peru, Acuña played in local bands such as La Orquesta de los Hermanos Neciosup[[2]](#cite_note-2) from the age of ten. Acuña then followed his brothers and moved to [Lima](/wiki/Lima "Lima") as a teenager. At the age of eighteen he joined the band of [Perez Prado](/wiki/Perez_Prado "Perez Prado"), and in 1965 he moved to [San Juan, Puerto Rico](/wiki/San_Juan,_Puerto_Rico "San Juan, Puerto Rico"). In 1974 Acuña moved to [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas,_Nevada "Las Vegas, Nevada"), working with artists such as [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley"), [The Temptations](/wiki/The_Temptations "The Temptations"), and [Diana Ross](/wiki/Diana_Ross "Diana Ross"), and the following year he joined the jazz-fusion group [Weather Report](/wiki/Weather_Report "Weather Report"), appearing on the albums *[Black Market](/wiki/Black_Market_(Weather_Report_album) "Black Market (Weather Report album)")* and *[Heavy Weather](/wiki/Heavy_Weather_(album) "Heavy Weather (album)")*. While in New York City, Acuña recorded several songs under [RCA records](/wiki/RCA_Records "RCA Records"). Acuña decided to leave because of the genre limitations placed on him, in which [RCA records](/wiki/RCA_Records "RCA Records") only had him play Latin music.

Acuña left Weather Report in 1978, and became a session musician in California, recording and playing live with (amongst many others) [Paul McCartney](/wiki/Paul_McCartney "Paul McCartney"), [Joni Mitchell](/wiki/Joni_Mitchell "Joni Mitchell"), [Ella Fitzgerald](/wiki/Ella_Fitzgerald "Ella Fitzgerald"), [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley"), [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson"), [Chick Corea](/wiki/Chick_Corea "Chick Corea"), [Whitney Houston](/wiki/Whitney_Houston "Whitney Houston"), [Plácido Domingo](/wiki/Pl%C3%A1cido_Domingo "Plácido Domingo"), former Weather Report bandmates [Wayne Shorter](/wiki/Wayne_Shorter "Wayne Shorter") and [Joe Zawinul](/wiki/Joe_Zawinul "Joe Zawinul"), [Herbie Hancock](/wiki/Herbie_Hancock "Herbie Hancock"), [Carlos Santana](/wiki/Carlos_Santana "Carlos Santana"), [Antonio Carlos Jobim](/wiki/Antonio_Carlos_Jobim "Antonio Carlos Jobim"), [Beck](/wiki/Beck "Beck"), [Roberta Flack](/wiki/Roberta_Flack "Roberta Flack"), [U2](/wiki/U2 "U2"), [Al Jarreau](/wiki/Al_Jarreau "Al Jarreau") and [Marcos Witt](/wiki/Marcos_Witt "Marcos Witt"). He appears on recordings by musicians as culturally diverse as [Lee Ritenour](/wiki/Lee_Ritenour "Lee Ritenour"), [Johnny Clegg](/wiki/Johnny_Clegg "Johnny Clegg"), [Roy Orbison](/wiki/Roy_Orbison "Roy Orbison"), [YellowJackets](/wiki/YellowJackets "YellowJackets"), [Lalo Schifrin](/wiki/Lalo_Schifrin "Lalo Schifrin"), [Milton Nascimento](/wiki/Milton_Nascimento "Milton Nascimento"), [Don Grusin](/wiki/Don_Grusin "Don Grusin"), [Dave Grusin](/wiki/Dave_Grusin "Dave Grusin"), The [Brecker Brothers](/wiki/Brecker_Brothers "Brecker Brothers"), [Arturo Sandoval](/wiki/Arturo_Sandoval "Arturo Sandoval"), [Vladislav Sendecki](/wiki/Vladislav_Sendecki "Vladislav Sendecki"), [Paquito d'Rivera](/wiki/Paquito_d%27Rivera "Paquito d'Rivera"), [Gonzalo Rubalcaba](/wiki/Gonzalo_Rubalcaba "Gonzalo Rubalcaba"), [Brad Mehldau](/wiki/Brad_Mehldau "Brad Mehldau"), [BoDeans](/wiki/BoDeans "BoDeans"), [Paco de Lucia](/wiki/Paco_de_Luc%C3%ADa "Paco de Lucía"), [John Patitucci](/wiki/John_Patitucci "John Patitucci"), [Sadao Watanabe](/wiki/Sadao_Watanabe_(musician) "Sadao Watanabe (musician)"), [Lyle Mays](/wiki/Lyle_Mays "Lyle Mays"), [Diana Ross](/wiki/Diana_Ross "Diana Ross"), [Sérgio Mendes](/wiki/S%C3%A9rgio_Mendes "Sérgio Mendes"), [Robbie Robertson](/wiki/Robbie_Robertson "Robbie Robertson"), [Jackson Browne](/wiki/Jackson_Browne "Jackson Browne"), [Bette Midler](/wiki/Bette_Midler "Bette Midler"), [Jennifer Nettles](/wiki/Jennifer_Nettles "Jennifer Nettles"), [Christina Aguilera](/wiki/Christina_Aguilera "Christina Aguilera"), [Seal](/wiki/Seal_(musician) "Seal (musician)") and [Chris Botti](/wiki/Chris_Botti "Chris Botti").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In the 1980s Acuña also recorded and toured with the Christian jazz band [Koinonia](/wiki/Koinonia_(band) "Koinonia (band)"), which featured session musicians [Abraham Laboriel](/wiki/Abraham_Laboriel "Abraham Laboriel"), [Justo Almario](/wiki/Justo_Almario "Justo Almario"), Hadley Hockensmith, Harlan Rogers, and Bill Maxwell. He played on [Willy DeVille](/wiki/Willy_DeVille "Willy DeVille")'s *[Crow Jane Alley](/wiki/Crow_Jane_Alley "Crow Jane Alley")* album and in 1987 he teamed up with [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley")'s [TCB Band](/wiki/TCB_Band "TCB Band") for the [Roy Orbison](/wiki/Roy_Orbison "Roy Orbison") TV special "[A Black and White Night](/wiki/Roy_Orbison_and_Friends,_A_Black_and_White_Night "Roy Orbison and Friends, A Black and White Night")". He played percussion on [Blondie](/wiki/Blondie_(band) "Blondie (band)")'s number one hit "[The Tide Is High](/wiki/The_Tide_Is_High "The Tide Is High")" and also recorded more than 300 movies under the direction of Lalo Schifrin, Dave Grusin, [Michel Legrand](/wiki/Michel_Legrand "Michel Legrand"), [Bill Conti](/wiki/Bill_Conti "Bill Conti"), [James Horner](/wiki/James_Horner "James Horner"), [James Newton Howard](/wiki/James_Newton_Howard "James Newton Howard"), [John Williams](/wiki/John_Williams "John Williams"), [Alan Silvestri](/wiki/Alan_Silvestri "Alan Silvestri"), [Michael Giacchino](/wiki/Michael_Giacchino "Michael Giacchino"), Christopher Beck, [Maurice Jarre](/wiki/Maurice_Jarre "Maurice Jarre"), [Steve Jablonsky](/wiki/Steve_Jablonsky "Steve Jablonsky"), [John Powell](/wiki/John_Powell_(film_composer) "John Powell (film composer)") and [Heitor Pereira](/wiki/Heitor_Pereira "Heitor Pereira"). In 1987, Acuña was summoned back to Perú by producer Ricardo Ghibellini to be the musical producer of Los Hijos del Sol, a group of Peruvians designed to promote Peruvian music worldwide.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
